reasoning: According to the current Wikipedia article "List of infectious diseases" (as of October 2025), there is no vaccine listed for Gonorrhea. For these probabilities to resolve to Yes, a vaccine would need to be approved and subsequently added to this specific Wikipedia page within the very short time horizons (ranging from 1 week to 3 months away). The development, approval, and distribution of vaccines take years, and while there are ongoing research efforts (such as the meningitis B vaccine showing some cross-protection and other early-stage trials), there is no dedicated gonorrhea vaccine on the verge of official approval and widespread recognition such that it would be added to this Wikipedia table by early 2026. Therefore, the chance of this changing in the next 1 to 3 months is extremely low. Uncertainty slightly increases for further out dates but remains well below 10%.
